qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated server-side request forgery via LiveLinks proxy endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes SSRF via the downloadURL parameter in aVideoEncoder.json.php, which is a different endpoint, different file, different parameter, and different authentication requirement (authenticated vs unauthenticated). While both are SSRF in the same project, the root cause locations are distinct.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated SQL injection via catName parameter in video category filter
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes SSRF (CWE-918) while this finding is SQL injection, an entirely different vulnerability class with no shared root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory describes authenticated SSRF via the downloadURL parameter in aVideoEncoder.json.php. The ssrf_livelinks_proxy finding describes a different SSRF in plugin/LiveLinks/proxy.php via the livelink parameter (different endpoint, parameter, and authentication requirement). The sqli_catname finding is SQL injection, an unrelated vulnerability class.
evidence: Advisory specifies aVideoEncoder.json.php with downloadURL parameter as the vulnerable endpoint
ssrf_livelinks_proxy identifies plugin/LiveLinks/proxy.php with livelink parameter — different file and parameter
sqli_catname identifies SQL injection in objects/video.php — different vulnerability class
